context: pa_concern HIGH+MED (224 products) already applied. 729 LOW-confidence products remain unassigned. LOW means the script fell back to a category-default concern (e.g. any Korean skincare → dryness-hydration). These are probably correct for most products. Owner (Warlord) has approved: apply all LOW-confidence rows as-is — the category default is acceptable at this stage.

steps.action: Build a new approved CSV for LOW-confidence rows only
steps.how: Read pa-concern-dry-run-20260521-171429.csv. For rows where confidence=LOW keep concerns_assigned as-is. For confidence=SKIP or HIGH or MED set concerns_assigned=SKIP (already done, double-guard). Write to /tmp/pa-concern-low-approved.csv
python3 pa-concern-apply.py /tmp/pa-concern-low-approved.csv --dry-run (if the script supports it) or inspect the CSV counts manually

wp --path=/var/www/wordpress --allow-root eval 'global $wpdb; $r = $wpdb->get_var("SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT p.ID) FROM wp_posts p LEFT JOIN wp_term_relationships tr ON p.ID=tr.object_id LEFT JOIN wp_term_taxonomy tt ON tr.term_taxonomy_id=tt.term_taxonomy_id WHERE p.post_status=\"publish\" AND p.post_type=\"product\" AND tt.taxonomy IS NULL OR tt.taxonomy!=\"pa_concern\""); echo $r;' — count should drop significantly

output: Report: how many LOW rows applied, how many were skipped (already had concern), remaining without concern.
constraints: Apply script is additive-only — never removes existing pa_concern
Do not touch HIGH or MED rows — already applied
Do not modify the apply script itself
Do not touch any Next.js files
Save rollback SQL before applying (the script does this automatically)
